function.name: read_folder

path.description: Path to the folder (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth when recursive is true. Default: 3

recursive.description: If true, list subdirectories recursively. Default: false

include_hidden.description: Include hidden files (starting with .). Default: false

filter_extensions.description: Only include files with these extensions (e.g., [".js", ".ts"])

function.description: List files and subdirectories in a folder on the local filesystem. Returns metadata including names, paths, types, sizes, and modification times.

function.name: read_file

end_line.description: Read to this line number (1-indexed). Useful for large files.

file_path.description: Path to the file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

start_line.description: Read from this line number (1-indexed). Useful for large files.

function.description: Read the contents of a text file from the local filesystem. Supports encoding options and line ranges for large files.

function.name: write_file

path.description: Path to the file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: The content to write to the file.

overwrite.description: This flags describes whether an existing file will be overwritten. If the file does not exist, this flag has no effect. If the file exists, this flag must be TRUE to over-write the file. If the file exists and this flag is FALSE, the tool function will not write the contents and will return an error. Default is FALSE.

function.description: Create a new file or update an existing file with the specified content. Use overwrite=true to replace existing files.

function.name: windows_shell

command.description: The Windows command to execute. Allowed commands: dir, del, move, rename, type, echo, cd, tree, findstr, where, ver, hostname, ipconfig, ping, tracert, nslookup, systeminfo, tasklist, whoami, git, gh, slack

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the command (e.g., "/s /b" for dir, "C:" for cd)

function.description: Execute Windows command prompt commands. Only specific whitelisted commands are allowed for security. Returns the command output as a string.

function.name: unix_shell

command.description: The Unix shell command to execute. Allowed commands: ls, cat, echo, pwd, cd, tree, grep, find, which, uname, hostname, ifconfig, ip, ping, traceroute, nslookup, dig, ps, whoami, git, gh, slack

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the command (e.g., "-la" for ls, "/etc/hosts" for cat)

function.description: Execute Unix/Linux/macOS shell commands. Only specific whitelisted commands are allowed for security. Returns the command output as a string.

function.name: read_word

file_path.description: Path to the .docx file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

function.description: Read the contents of a Microsoft Word (.docx) file and extract plain text. Only supports .docx format (not legacy .doc files).

function.name: read_excel

file_path.description: Path to the .xlsx file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

sheet_name.description: Optional: Specific sheet name to read. If not provided, all sheets will be read.

function.description: Read the contents of a Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) file and extract data as text. Only supports .xlsx format (not legacy .xls files). Returns data from all sheets in CSV format.

function.name: read_pdf

file_path.description: Path to the .pdf file (can be relative to working directory or absolute)

function.description: Read the contents of a PDF file and extract plain text. Works with standard PDF files.

content:
Great — let’s design a **Gym Management System model** for your gym service.

I’ll structure this in a practical way so you can use it for:
- ✅ Database design  
- ✅ App development  
- ✅ Business planning  
- ✅ System documentation  

---

# 1️⃣ Define the Type of Gym System

First, clarify your gym service type:

- 🏋️ Regular gym (monthly memberships)
- 🧑‍🏫 Personal training focused
- 🧘 Classes-based gym (Zumba, Yoga, CrossFit)
- 🏢 Multi-branch gym
- 📱 Online + physical hybrid

For now, I’ll design a **complete scalable model** that works for most gyms.

---

# 2️⃣ Core System Modules

Your gym system should have these modules:

1. Member Management  
2. Membership Plans  
3. Payments & Billing  
4. Trainers Management  
5. Class Scheduling  
6. Attendance Tracking  
7. Equipment Management  
8. Reports & Analytics  
9. Staff & Roles  

---

# 3️⃣ Database Model (Entity-Relationship Structure)

Here is a clean system model (database-level design):

---

## 👤 1. Members Table

**Members**
- member_id (PK)
- first_name
- last_name
- gender
- date_of_birth
- phone
- email
- address
- emergency_contact_name
- emergency_contact_phone
- join_date
- status (Active / Inactive / Suspended)
- photo

---

## 💳 2. Membership Plans

**Membership_Plans**
- plan_id (PK)
- plan_name (Monthly, 3 Months, Annual)
- duration_in_days
- price
- description
- access_level (Gym Only / Gym + Classes / VIP)
- is_active

---

## 📄 3. Member Subscriptions

(This links members to plans)

**Subscriptions**
- subscription_id (PK)
- member_id (FK)
- plan_id (FK)
- start_date
- end_date
- amount_paid
- payment_status (Paid / Partial / Pending)
- created_at

---

## 💰 4. Payments

**Payments**
- payment_id (PK)
- member_id (FK)
- subscription_id (FK)
- amount
- payment_method (Cash / Card / Mobile Money)
- transaction_reference
- payment_date
- received_by (staff_id)

---

## 🧑‍🏫 5. Trainers

**Trainers**
- trainer_id (PK)
- first_name
- last_name
- specialization
- phone
- email
- hire_date
- salary_type (Fixed / Commission)
- status

---

## 🏋️ 6. Personal Training Sessions

**PT_Sessions**
- session_id (PK)
- member_id (FK)
- trainer_id (FK)
- session_date
- session_time
- duration
- notes
- status (Scheduled / Completed / Cancelled)

---

## 🧘 7. Classes

**Classes**
- class_id (PK)
- class_name (Zumba, Yoga, HIIT)
- trainer_id (FK)
- capacity
- schedule_time
- duration
- room

---

## 📋 8. Class Bookings

**Class_Bookings**
- booking_id (PK)
- member_id (FK)
- class_id (FK)
- booking_date
- attendance_status

---

## ✅ 9. Attendance Tracking

**Attendance**
- attendance_id (PK)
- member_id (FK)
- check_in_time
- check_out_time
- recorded_by

---

## 🏢 10. Staff (Reception/Admin)

**Staff**
- staff_id (PK)
- name
- role (Admin / Receptionist / Manager)
- username
- password_hash
- status

---

## 🏋️ Equipment

**Equipment**
- equipment_id (PK)
- name
- category
- purchase_date
- condition
- maintenance_schedule
- status

---

# 4️⃣ System Architecture Model

You can build it as:

### Option A: Simple System
- Frontend (Web or Mobile App)
- Backend API (Node.js / Django / Laravel)
- Database (MySQL / PostgreSQL)

### Option B: Advanced System
- Web Admin Panel
- Member Mobile App
- QR Code Check-in
- Payment Gateway Integration
- SMS Reminders
- Auto-expiry notifications

---

# 5️⃣ Business Logic Model

Here’s how your system should behave:

### ✅ When a new member joins:
1. Create Member record
2. Assign Membership Plan
3. Create Subscription
4. Record Payment
5. Generate Receipt
6. Activate membership

---

### ✅ When membership expires:
- Automatically update status to "Expired"
- Deny check-in
- Send SMS reminder

---

### ✅ When member checks in:
- Validate active subscription
- Record attendance
- Allow entry

---

# 6️⃣ Reports Your Gym System Should Generate

- Daily revenue report
- Monthly income report
- Active members list
- Expired memberships
- Trainer performance report
- Class attendance report
- Peak gym hours report

---

# 7️⃣ Simple ER Diagram Structure (Text Version)

```
Members ───< Subscriptions >─── Membership_Plans
   │
   ├──< Payments
   ├──< Attendance
   ├──< PT_Sessions >── Trainers
   └──< Class_Bookings >── Classes ─── Trainers
```

---

# 8️⃣ If You Want a More Advanced Model

You can add:

- ✅ Loyalty points
- ✅ Referral system
- ✅ Nutrition tracking
- ✅ Body measurement tracking
- ✅ Workout plan assignment
- ✅ Multi-branch support
- ✅ Inventory for supplements

---

# 9️⃣ Next Step

To customize this perfectly for your gym, tell me:

1. Is your gym single branch or multi-branch?
2. Do you offer classes?
3. Do you offer personal training?
4. Do you want a mobile app?
5. Are you building this yourself or hiring a developer?

I can then:
- ✅ Create a simplified model
- ✅ Create SQL schema
- ✅ Create a system flowchart
- ✅ Create a business operations model
- ✅ Or design it for a specific tech stack

Let’s build this properly 🚀
